sections.paragraph:
fut. יִצְלַח-

(1) to go over or through (as a river), followed by an acc. 2Sa 19:18. (Compare צֵלָחָה.)

(2) to attack, to fall upon, used of the Spirit of Jehovah falling upon a man; followed by עַל Jud. 14:19 15:14 1Sa 10:10 11:6 followed by אֶל 1Sa 16:13 18:10 used of God himself breaking forth upon men, poet. followed by an acc. Amo 5:6. (Compare בֹּוא No. 2, d.)

(3) to go on well, to prosper, to succeed as an affair, (comp. Germ. durchgehn, Fortgang haben; Lat. lex perfertur; Heb. אָשַׁר, כָּשַׁר), Isa 53:10 comp. 54:17 used of a man in an affair, Psa 45:5 Jer 22:30 to flourish, of a plant (fortkommen), Eze 17:9, 10 Eze 17:10. Followed by לְ to prosper for anything, to be fit for anything, Jer 13:7, 10 Jer 13:10. (Arab. صَلُحَ to be fit.)

Hiphil-

(1) trans. to make successful, to prosper-(a) any one’s affair, Gen 24:21, 56 Gen 24:56 39:3 , 23 Gen 39:23.-(b) any person, followed by an acc. of person, 2Ch 26:5 followed by לְ Neh 1:11 2:20 .

(2) to accomplish prosperously, to finish well, 2Ch 7:11 Dan 8:25 especially with the nouns דַּרְכֹּו, דְּרָכָיו to make one’s way or counsel prosper, to be successful, Deu 28:29. Psa 37:7, מַצְלִיחַ דַּרְכֹּו “one who is successful in all things.” Hence without the acc. to be successful, as an undertaking, Jud. 18:5 as a person in any undertaking, 1Ki 22:12, 15 1Ki 22:15 1Ch 22:13 29:23 2Ch 18:14 Pro 28:13 Jer 2:37.
